title: Open models, frontier quality, 90% lower cost | Together AI
gist: Together AI's marketing page argues that top open-weight models now match closed models on key benchmarks at 90% lower cost, citing GLM-5.2 ranking #1 in Design Arena and #2 in Code Arena at $1.40/1M tokens vs. ~$10 for the top closed model, and customer case studies showing 4-6x cost reductions versus GPT-5 and Opus — with a drop-in OpenAI-compatible API enabling zero-code migration.

themes.theme: Quality Parity Between Open and Closed Models

insights.claim: GLM-5.2 ranks second in Code Arena WebDev at $1.40/1M tokens versus approximately $10 for the top closed model — a 7x cost advantage — while ranking first in the human-judged Design Arena coding category ahead of Claude Fable 5 and all Opus variants.
insights.speaker: Together AI Blog
insights.so_what: GLM-5.2's benchmark leadership in Design Arena at 7x lower cost per token than closed models represents a concrete switching argument for enterprises running design or web generation workloads.
insights.quote: GLM-5.2 ranks second at $1.40, against $10 for the top closed model. GLM-5.2 takes the top spot, ahead of Claude Fable 5 and every Opus variant.

insights.claim: GLM-5.2 scores 51 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index, placing open weights 'right behind the closed leaders' — marking the point where open model quality has crossed the threshold for enterprise substitution on general intelligence tasks.
insights.speaker: Together AI Blog
insights.so_what: An open model within one threshold step of closed leaders on a composite intelligence benchmark is an inflection point for enterprise procurement; procurement teams should re-evaluate closed API contracts against current open-model benchmarks.
insights.quote: At 51, GLM-5.2 puts open weights right behind the closed leaders.

insights.claim: On SWE-bench Pro (agentic software engineering), GLM-5.2 leads all open models and outperforms GPT-5.5, placing just below Claude Opus 4.8 — establishing open models as production-viable for autonomous coding agent workloads.
insights.speaker: Together AI Blog
insights.so_what: SWE-bench Pro performance near Opus 4.8 at a fraction of the cost makes GLM-5.2 a credible replacement for the dominant closed model in coding agent pipelines — a large and fast-growing workload category.
insights.quote: GLM-5.2 leads every open model on SWE-bench Pro — above GPT-5.5 and just behind Claude Opus 4.8.

insights.claim: Decagon, a voice AI company, achieved 6x lower cost per query versus GPT-5 mini by switching to open models on Together AI, while also achieving sub-400ms p95 model latency — demonstrating that open-model cost savings are achievable at production scale and latency requirements.
insights.speaker: Together AI Blog
insights.so_what: Voice AI is latency-constrained and margin-sensitive; a 6x cost reduction at sub-400ms p95 makes open models financially compelling for the fastest-growing AI application category.
insights.quote: Decagon unlocked 6x lower cost per query than GPT-5 mini.

insights.claim: Yutori achieved 4-5x cost reduction and 2x faster inference versus frontier closed models by running open models on Together AI, enabling deployment in both consumer background agents and developer workflow integrations.
insights.speaker: Together AI Blog
insights.so_what: 4-5x cost reduction at 2x faster inference reframes the open-model migration decision from a quality risk to a clear ROI calculation; enterprises that haven't evaluated open models in the past 6 months are likely using outdated quality assumptions.
insights.quote: Working in collaboration with Together AI, we've been able to bring Yutori's products not just to consumers using background agents, but also to developers who are hooking up to their workflows.

insights.claim: GLM-5.2 generated a web design landing page for $0.06 versus $0.49 for Claude Opus 4.8 for the same prompt — a 6x+ cost difference with results described as 'nearly identical' in quality — illustrating per-task economics that compress AI margin at scale.
insights.speaker: Together AI Blog
insights.so_what: At 8x cost differential per web design task, even a small quality gap in favor of open models would be economically decisive; enterprises running high-volume generation workloads should run head-to-head cost audits.
insights.quote: GLM-5.2 cost just $0.06 against $0.49 for Opus: over 6× cheaper, and faster and more token-efficient.

themes.theme: Coding Agent Cost Economics: Kimi K2.7 vs. Opus 4.8

insights.claim: Kimi K2.7 Code costs 70% less than Claude Opus 4.8 for production coding agent workloads — making it a direct substitution candidate for the most prevalent enterprise agentic use case.
insights.speaker: Together AI Blog
insights.so_what: Coding agents are the primary enterprise AI workload; a 70% cost reduction for a competitive-quality model has direct P&L impact for software companies running AI-assisted development at scale.
insights.quote: Kimi K2.7 Code costs 70% less than Opus 4.8 for production coding agent workloads.

themes.theme: Data Sovereignty and IP Control as Switching Argument

insights.claim: Together AI positions open-model ownership — holding weights, running on domestic US infrastructure, SOC 2 Type 2 and ISO 27001:2022 compliance, no training on customer data — as a distinct advantage over closed model APIs where data control is contractually constrained.
insights.speaker: Together AI Blog
insights.so_what: Data sovereignty requirements in regulated industries (finance, healthcare, defense) are an underweighted driver of open-model adoption; procurement decisions in these sectors are not purely price-driven and favor auditable domestic infrastructure.
insights.quote: You own the weights and your IP, your data stays yours, and the model runs on domestic infrastructure you control. Together AI is SOC 2 Type 2 and ISO 27001:2022 compliant.

insights.claim: Together AI exposes an OpenAI-compatible API, enabling migration from closed models to open models with only an endpoint URL change and no code modifications — eliminating the primary technical switching cost.
insights.speaker: Together AI Blog
insights.so_what: Zero-code migration removes the largest developer barrier to open-model switching; enterprises that have standardized on the OpenAI API format can switch inference providers without engineering work — accelerating competitive disruption of closed model revenue.
insights.quote: It is a drop-in switch. Together exposes an OpenAI-compatible API, so you point your existing client at our endpoint and run the top open models with no code changes.
